017package org.apache.juneau.rest.client.classic;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.httppart.HttpPartType.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ClassUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.IoUtils.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
025import static org.apache.juneau.http.classic.HttpEntities.*;
026import static org.apache.juneau.http.classic.HttpHeaders.*;
027import static org.apache.juneau.rest.RestSharedConstants.*;
028import static org.apache.juneau.rest.client.classic.RestOperation.*;
029
030import java.io.*;
031import java.lang.reflect.*;
032import java.net.*;
033import java.text.*;
034import java.util.*;
035import java.util.concurrent.*;
036import java.util.function.*;
037import java.util.logging.*;
038
039import org.apache.http.*;
040import org.apache.http.ParseException;
041import org.apache.http.client.config.*;
042import org.apache.http.client.entity.*;
043import org.apache.http.client.methods.*;
044import org.apache.http.client.utils.*;
045import org.apache.http.concurrent.*;
046import org.apache.http.entity.BasicHttpEntity;
047import org.apache.http.params.*;
048import org.apache.http.protocol.*;
049import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
050import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
051import org.apache.juneau.commons.httppart.*;
052import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
053import org.apache.juneau.http.classic.*;
054import org.apache.juneau.http.classic.HttpHeaders;
055import org.apache.juneau.http.classic.entity.*;
056import org.apache.juneau.http.classic.header.*;
057import org.apache.juneau.http.classic.header.ContentType;
058import org.apache.juneau.http.classic.part.*;
059import org.apache.juneau.http.classic.resource.*;
060import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
061import org.apache.juneau.marshall.bson.*;
062import org.apache.juneau.marshall.html.*;
063import org.apache.juneau.marshall.httppart.*;
064import org.apache.juneau.marshall.jcs.*;
065import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
066import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json5.*;
067import org.apache.juneau.marshall.marshaller.*;
068import org.apache.juneau.marshall.marshaller.Uon;
069import org.apache.juneau.marshall.msgpack.*;
070import org.apache.juneau.marshall.oapi.*;
071import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
072import org.apache.juneau.marshall.plaintext.*;
073import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
074import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
075import org.apache.juneau.marshall.uon.*;
076import org.apache.juneau.marshall.urlencoding.*;
077import org.apache.juneau.marshall.xml.*;
078
079/**
080 * Represents a request to a remote REST resource.
081 *
082 * <p>
083 * Instances of this class are created by the various creator methods on the {@link RestClient} class.
084 *
085 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
086 * <p class='bjava'>
087 *    <jc>// Create a request and automatically close it.</jc>
088 *    <jk>try</jk> (<jv>RestRequest</jv> <jv>req</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<js>"/myResource"</js>)) {
089 *       <jv>req</jv>
090 *          .queryData(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>)
091 *          .run()
092 *          .assertStatus().asCode().is(200);
093 *    }
094 * </p>
095 *
096 * <p>
097 * The {@link #close()} method will automatically close any associated {@link RestResponse} if one was created via {@link #run()}.
098 *
099 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
100 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe.
101 *    <li class='note'>This class implements {@link AutoCloseable} and can be used in try-with-resources blocks.
102 *       The {@link #close()} method allows unchecked exceptions to propagate for debuggability,
103 *       while catching and logging checked exceptions to follow AutoCloseable best practices.
104 * </ul>
105 *
106 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
107 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauRestClient">juneau-rest-client Basics</a>
108 * </ul>
109 */
110@SuppressWarnings({
111   "java:S115",  // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase naming convention
112   "resource",   // Resource management handled externally
113   "java:S3740", // Raw Class/Supplier types used for fluent REST request building where response type is unknown at construction time
114   "java:S1133"  // Intentional deprecation retained for backward compatibility until the documented removal; the reminder is not actionable now.
115})
116public class RestRequest extends MarshallingSession implements HttpUriRequest, Configurable, AutoCloseable {
117
118   // Property name constants
119   private static final String PROP_ignoreErrors = "ignoreErrors";
120   private static final String PROP_interceptors = "interceptors";
121   private static final String PROP_requestBodySchema = "requestBodySchema";
122   private static final String PROP_response = "response";
123   private static final String PROP_serializer = "serializer";
124
125   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
126   private static final String ARG_client = "client";
127   private static final String ARG_method = "method";
128   private static final String ARG_parser = "parser";
129   private static final String ARG_uri = "uri";
130   private static final String ARG_value = "value";
131   private static final String ARG_interceptors = "interceptors";
132
133   // URI path separator (always "/" per RFC 3986)
134   private static final String URI_PATH_SEPARATOR = "/";
135
136   private class SimpleFormData extends SimplePart {
137      SimpleFormData(NameValuePair x) {
138         super(x, client.isSkipEmptyFormData());
139      }
140   }
141
142   private class SimpleHeader extends SimplePart implements Header {
143
144      SimpleHeader(NameValuePair x) {
145         super(x, client.isSkipEmptyHeaderData());
146      }
147
148      @Override
149      public HeaderElement[] getElements() throws ParseException { return new HeaderElement[0]; }
150   }
151
152   private class SimplePart implements NameValuePair {
153      final String name;
154      final String value;
155
156      SimplePart(NameValuePair x, boolean skipIfEmpty) {
157         name = x.getName();
158         if (x instanceof SerializedHeader x2) {
159            value = x2.copyWith(getPartSerializerSession(), null).getValue();
160         } else if (x instanceof SerializedPart x3) {
161            value = x3.copyWith(getPartSerializerSession(), null).getValue();
162         } else {
163            var v = x.getValue();
164            value = (ie(v) && skipIfEmpty) ? null : v;
165         }
166      }
167
168      @Override
169      public String getName() { return name; }
170
171      @Override
172      public String getValue() { return value; }
173
174      boolean isValid() {
175         return !(ie(name) || value == null);
176      }
177   }
178
179   private class SimplePath extends SimplePart {
180      SimplePath(NameValuePair x) {
181         super(x, false);
182      }
183   }
184
185   private class SimpleQuery extends SimplePart {
186      SimpleQuery(NameValuePair x) {
187         super(x, client.isSkipEmptyQueryData());
188      }
189   }
190
191   private static final ContentType TEXT_PLAIN = ContentType.TEXT_PLAIN;
192
193   private static boolean isHeaderArray(Object o) {
194      if (! isArray(o))
195         return false;
196      return Header.class.isAssignableFrom(o.getClass().getComponentType());
197   }
198
199   private static boolean isNameValuePairArray(Object o) {
200      if (! isArray(o))
201         return false;
202      return NameValuePair.class.isAssignableFrom(o.getClass().getComponentType());
203   }
204
205   @SuppressWarnings({
206      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
207   })
208   private static Map<Object,Object> toMap(Object o) {
209      return (Map<Object,Object>)o;
210   }
211
212   final RestClient client;                               // The client that created this call.
213   private final HttpRequestBase request;                 // The request.
214   private boolean ignoreErrors;
215   private boolean suppressLogging;
216   private HttpContext context;
217   private HttpHost target;
218   private HttpPartSchema contentSchema;
219   private HttpPartSerializerSession partSerializerSession;
220   private HeaderList headerData;
221   private List<Class<? extends Throwable>> rethrow;
222   List<RestCallInterceptor> interceptors = list();   // Used for intercepting and altering requests.
223   private Object content;
224   private Parser parser;
225   private PartList formData;
226   private PartList pathData;
227   private PartList queryData;
228   private Predicate<Integer> errorCodes;
229   private RestResponse response;                         // The response.
230   private Serializer serializer;
231   private URIBuilder uriBuilder;
232
233   private final Map<HttpPartSerializer,HttpPartSerializerSession> partSerializerSessions = new IdentityHashMap<>();
234
235   /**
236    * Constructs a REST call with the specified method name.
237    *
238    * @param client The client that created this request.
239    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
240    * @param uri The target URI.
241    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
242    * @param method The HTTP method name (uppercase).
243    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
244    * @param hasBody Whether this method has a body.
245    * @throws RestCallException If an exception or non-200 response code occurred during the connection attempt.
246    */
247   protected RestRequest(RestClient client, URI uri, String method, boolean hasBody) throws RestCallException {
248      super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_client, client).getMarshallingContext().createSession());
249      this.client = client;
250      this.request = createInnerRequest(assertArgNotNull(ARG_method, method), assertArgNotNull(ARG_uri, uri), hasBody);
251      this.errorCodes = client.errorCodes;
252      this.formData = client.createFormData();
253      this.headerData = client.createHeaderData();
254      this.ignoreErrors = client.ignoreErrors;
255      this.pathData = client.createPathData();
256      this.queryData = client.createQueryData();
257      this.uriBuilder = new URIBuilder(request.getURI());
258   }
259
260   /**
261    * Aborts this http request. Any active execution of this method should return immediately.
262    *
263    * If the request has not started, it will abort after the next execution.
264    * <br>Aborting this request will cause all subsequent executions with this request to fail.
265    */
266   @Override /* Overridden from HttpUriRequest */
267   public void abort() throws UnsupportedOperationException {
268      request.abort();
269   }
270
271   /**
272    * Sets the value for the <c>Accept</c> request header.
273    *
274    * <p>
275    * This overrides the media type specified on the parser, but is overridden by calling
276    * <code>header(<js>"Accept"</js>, value);</code>
277    *
278    * @param value The new header value.
279    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (header will not be set).
280    * @return This object.
281    * @throws RestCallException Invalid input.
282    */
283   public RestRequest accept(String value) throws RestCallException {
284      return header(Accept.of(value));
285   }
286
287   /**
288    * Sets the value for the <c>Accept-Charset</c> request header.
289    *
290    * <p>
291    * This is a shortcut for calling <code>header(<js>"Accept-Charset"</js>, value);</code>
292    *
293    * @param value The new header value.
294    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (header will not be set).
295    * @return This object.
296    * @throws RestCallException Invalid input.
297    */
298   public RestRequest acceptCharset(String value) throws RestCallException {
299      return header(AcceptCharset.of(value));
300   }
301
302   /**
303    * Adds a header to this message.
304    *
305    * The header will be appended to the end of the list.
306    *
307    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
308    *    <li class='note'>{@link #header(Header)} is an equivalent method and the preferred method for fluent-style coding.
309    * </ul>
310    *
311    * @param header The header to append.
312    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (ignored).
313    */
314   @Override /* Overridden from HttpMessage */
315   public void addHeader(Header header) {
316      headerData.append(header);
317   }
318
319   /**
320    * Adds a header to this message.
321    *
322    * The header will be appended to the end of the list.
323    *
324    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
325    *    <li class='note'>{@link #header(String,Object)} is an equivalent method and the preferred method for fluent-style coding.
326    * </ul>
327    *
328    * @param name The name of the header.
329    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk> or empty (header will not be created).
330    * @param value The value of the header.
331    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
332    */
333   @Override /* Overridden from HttpMessage */
334   public void addHeader(String name, String value) {
335      headerData.append(stringHeader(name, value));
336   }
337
338   /**
339    * Sets {@link Cancellable} for the ongoing operation.
340    *
341    * @param cancellable The cancellable object.
342    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (no cancellation will be available).
343    * @return This object.
344    */
345   public RestRequest cancellable(Cancellable cancellable) {
346      request.setCancellable(cancellable);
347      return this;
348   }
349
350   /**
351    * Closes this request and its associated response (if one was created).
352    *
353    * <p>
354    * This method is idempotent and can be called multiple times without side effects.
355    *
356    * <h5 class='section'>Implementation Notes:</h5>
357    * <p>
358    * This implementation represents a compromise between strict AutoCloseable compliance and debuggability:
359    * <ul>
360    *    <li>Unchecked exceptions ({@link RuntimeException} and {@link Error}) from the response close are allowed to propagate.
361    *       This ensures programming errors and serious issues are visible during development and testing.
362    *    <li>Checked exceptions (including {@link RestCallException}) are caught and logged but not thrown.
363    *       This follows AutoCloseable best practices and prevents close exceptions from interfering with
364    *       try-with-resources cleanup or masking the original exception.
365    * </ul>
366    */
367   @Override /* Overridden from AutoCloseable */
368   public void close() {
369      try {
370         if (nn(response)) {
371            response.close();
372         }
373      } catch (RuntimeException e) {
374         // Let unchecked exceptions propagate for debuggability
375         throw e;
376      } catch (Exception e) {
377         // Log checked exceptions but don't throw - follows AutoCloseable best practices
378         client.log(Level.WARNING, e, "Error closing RestResponse");
379      }
380   }
381
382   /**
383    * Same as {@link #run()} but immediately calls {@link RestResponse#consume()} to clean up the response.
384    *
385    * <p>
386    * Use this method if you're only interested in the status line of the response and not the response entity.
387    * Attempts to call any of the methods on the response object that retrieve the body (e.g. {@link ResponseContent#asReader()}
388    * will cause a {@link RestCallException} to be thrown.
389    *
390    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
391    *    <li class='note'>You do not need to execute {@link InputStream#close()} on the response body to consume the response.
392    * </ul>
393    *
394    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
395    * <p class='bjava'>
396    *  <jc>// Get the response code.
397    *  // No need to call close() on the RestResponse object.</jc>
398    *  <jk>int</jk> <jv>rc</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<jsf>URI</jsf>).complete().getResponseCode();
399    * </p>
400    *
401    * @return The response object.
402    * @throws RestCallException If an exception or non-200 response code occurred during the connection attempt.
403    */
404   public RestResponse complete() throws RestCallException {
405      return run().consume();
406   }
407
408   /**
409    * Used in combination with {@link #cancellable(Cancellable)}.
410    *
411    * @deprecated Since 10.0, for removal.
412    * @return This object.
413    */
414   @Deprecated(since = "10.0", forRemoval = true)
415   public RestRequest completed() {
416      request.completed();
417      return this;
418   }
419
420   /**
421    * Same as {@link #complete()} but allows you to run the call asynchronously.
422    *
423    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
424    * <p class='bjava'>
425    *    Future&lt;RestResponse&gt; <jv>future</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<jsf>URI</jsf>).completeFuture();
426    *
427    *    <jc>// Do some other stuff</jc>
428    *
429    *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>rc</jv> = <jv>future</jv>.get().getResponseStatus();
430    * </p>
431    *
432    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
433    *    <li class='note'>Use the {@link RestClient.Builder#executorService(ExecutorService, boolean)} method to customize the
434    *       executor service used for creating {@link Future Futures}.
435    *    <li class='note'>You do not need to execute {@link InputStream#close()} on the response body to consume the response.
436    * </ul>
437    *
438    * @return The HTTP status code.
439    * @throws RestCallException If the executor service was not defined.
440    */
441   public Future<RestResponse> completeFuture() throws RestCallException {
442      return client.getExecutorService().submit(this::complete);
443   }
444
445   /**
446    * Sets the actual request configuration.
447    *
448    * @param value The new value.
449    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (default configuration will be used).
450    * @return This object.
451    */
452   public RestRequest config(RequestConfig value) {
453      request.setConfig(value);
454      return this;
455   }
456
457   /**
458    * Checks if a certain header is present in this message.
459    *
460    * Header values are ignored.
461    *
462    * @param name The header name to check for.
463    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
464    * @return <jk>true</jk> if at least one header with this name is present.
465    */
466   @Override /* Overridden from HttpMessage */
467   public boolean containsHeader(String name) {
468      return headerData.contains(name);
469   }
470
471   /**
472    * Sets the body of this request.
473    *
474    * @param value
475    *    The input to be sent to the REST resource (only valid for PUT/POST/PATCH) requests.
476    *    <br>Can be of the following types:
477    *    <ul class='spaced-list'>
478    *       <li>
479    *          {@link Reader} - Raw contents of {@code Reader} will be serialized to remote resource.
480    *       <li>
481    *          {@link InputStream} - Raw contents of {@code InputStream} will be serialized to remote resource.
482    *       <li>
483    *          {@link HttpResource} - Raw contents will be serialized to remote resource.  Additional headers and media type will be set on request.
484    *       <li>
485    *          {@link HttpEntity}/{@link BasicHttpEntity} - Bypass Juneau serialization and pass HttpEntity directly to HttpClient.
486    *       <li>
487    *          {@link Object} - POJO to be converted to text using the {@link Serializer} registered with the
488    *          {@link RestClient}.
489    *       <li>
490    *          {@link PartList} - Converted to a URL-encoded FORM post.
491    *       <li>
492    *          A {@link Supplier} of anything on this list.
493    *    </ul>
494    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (no body will be sent).
495    * @return This object.
496    */
497   public RestRequest content(Object value) {
498      content = value;
499      return this;
500   }
501
502   /**
503    * Sets the body of this request.
504    *
505    * @param input
506    *    The input to be sent to the REST resource (only valid for PUT/POST/PATCH) requests.
507    *    <br>Can be of the following types:
508    *    <ul class='spaced-list'>
509    *       <li>
510    *          {@link Reader} - Raw contents of {@code Reader} will be serialized to remote resource.
511    *       <li>
512    *          {@link InputStream} - Raw contents of {@code InputStream} will be serialized to remote resource.
513    *       <li>
514    *          {@link HttpResource} - Raw contents will be serialized to remote resource.  Additional headers and media type will be set on request.
515    *       <li>
516    *          {@link HttpEntity}/{@link BasicHttpEntity} - Bypass Juneau serialization and pass HttpEntity directly to HttpClient.
517    *       <li>
518    *          {@link Object} - POJO to be converted to text using the {@link Serializer} registered with the
519    *          {@link RestClient}.
520    *       <li>
521    *          {@link PartList} - Converted to a URL-encoded FORM post.
522    *       <li>
523    *          A {@link Supplier} of anything on this list.
524    *    </ul>
525    * @param schema The schema object that defines the format of the output.
526    *    <ul>
527    *       <li>If <jk>null</jk>, defaults to {@link HttpPartSchema#DEFAULT}.
528    *       <li>Only used if serializer is schema-aware (e.g. {@link OpenApiSerializer}).
529    *    </ul>
530    * @return This object.
531    */
532   public RestRequest content(Object input, HttpPartSchema schema) {
533      this.content = input;
534      this.contentSchema = schema;
535      return this;
536   }
537
538   /**
539    * Sets the body of this request as straight text bypassing the serializer.
540    *
541    * <p class='bjava'>
542    *    <jv>client</jv>
543    *       .put(<js>"/foo"</js>)
544    *       .content(<jk>new</jk> StringReader(<js>"foo"</js>))
545    *       .contentType(<js>"text/foo"</js>)
546    *       .run();
547    *
548    * <jv>client</jv>
549    *       .put(<js>"/foo"</js>)
550    *       .bodyString(<js>"foo"</js>)
551    *       .run();
552    * </p>
553    *
554    * <p>
555    * Note that this is different than the following which will serialize <l>foo</l> as a JSON string <l>"foo"</l>.
556    * <p class='bjava'>
557    *    <jv>client</jv>
558    *       .put(<js>"/foo"</js>)
559    *       .json()
560    *       .content(<js>"foo"</js>)
561    *       .run();
562    * </p>
563    *
564    * @param input
565    *    The input to be sent to the REST resource (only valid for PUT/POST/PATCH) requests.
566    * @return This object.
567    * @throws RestCallException If a retry was attempted, but the entity was not repeatable.
568    */
569   public RestRequest contentString(Object input) throws RestCallException {
570      return content(input == null ? null : new StringReader(s(input)));
571   }
572
573   /**
574    * Sets the value for the <c>Content-Type</c> request header.
575    *
576    * <p>
577    * This overrides the media type specified on the serializer, but is overridden by calling
578    * <code>header(<js>"Content-Type"</js>, value);</code>
579    *
580    * @param value The new header value.
581    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (header will not be set).
582    * @return This object.
583    * @throws RestCallException Invalid input.
584    */
585   public RestRequest contentType(String value) throws RestCallException {
586      return header(ContentType.of(value));
587   }
588
589   /**
590    * Override the context to use for the execution.
591    *
592    * @param context The context to use for the execution, or <jk>null</jk> to use the default context.
593    * @return This object.
594    */
595   public RestRequest context(HttpContext context) {
596      this.context = context;
597      return this;
598   }
599
600   /**
601    * Sets <c>Debug: value</c> header on this request.
602    *
603    * @return This object.
604    * @throws RestCallException Invalid input.
605    */
606   public RestRequest debug() throws RestCallException {
607      header("Debug", true);
608      return this;
609   }
610
611   /**
612    * Allows you to override what status codes are considered error codes that would result in a {@link RestCallException}.
613    *
614    * <p>
615    * The default error code predicate is: <code>x -&gt; x &gt;= 400</code>.
616    *
617    * @param value The new predicate for calculating error codes.
618    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
619    * @return This object.
620    */
621   public RestRequest errorCodes(Predicate<Integer> value) {
622      errorCodes = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
623      return this;
624   }
625
626   /**
627    * Appends multiple form-data parameters to the request.
628    *
629    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
630    * <p class='bjava'>
631    *    <jc>// Appends two form-data parameters to the request.</jc>
632    *    <jv>client</jv>
633    *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
634    *       .formData(
635    *          BasicStringPart.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>),
636    *          BasicBooleanPart.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"baz"</js>, <jk>true</jk>)
637    *       )
638    *       .run();
639    * </p>
640    *
641    * @param parts
642    *    The parameters to set.
643    *    <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
644    * @return This object.
645    */
646   public RestRequest formData(NameValuePair...parts) {
647      formData.append(parts);
648      return this;
649   }
650
651   /**
652    * Adds a form-data parameter to the request body.
653    *
654    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
655    * <p class='bjava'>
656    *    <jc>// Adds form data parameter "foo=bar".</jc>
657    *    <jv>client</jv>
658    *       .formPost(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
659    *       .formData(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>)
660    *       .run();
661    * </p>
662    *
663    * @param name
664    *    The parameter name.
665    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk> or empty (parameter will not be created).
666    * @param value
667    *    The parameter value.
668    *    <br>Non-string values are converted to strings using the {@link HttpPartSerializer} defined on the client.
669    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
670    * @return This object.
671    */
672   public RestRequest formData(String name, Object value) {
673      formData.append(createPart(FORMDATA, name, value));
674      return this;
675   }
676
677   /**
678    * Appends multiple form-data parameters to the request from properties defined on a Java bean.
679    *
680    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
681    * <p class='bjava'>
682    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyFormData {
683    *       <jk>public</jk> String getFooBar() { <jk>return</jk> <js>"baz"</js>; }
684    *       <jk>public</jk> Integer getQux() { <jk>return</jk> 123; }
685    *    }
686    *
687    *    <jc>// Appends form data "fooBar=baz&amp;qux=123".</jc>
688    *    <jv>client</jv>
689    *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
690    *       .formDataBean(<jk>new</jk> MyFormData())
691    *       .run();
692    * </p>
693    *
694    * @param value The bean containing the properties to set as form-data parameter values.
695    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
696    * @return This object.
697    */
698   public RestRequest formDataBean(Object value) {
699      assertArg(isBean(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value)), "Object passed into formDataBean(Object) is not a bean.");
700      var b = formData;
701      toBeanMap(value).forEach((k, v) -> b.append(createPart(FORMDATA, k, v)));
702      return this;
703   }
704
705   /**
706    * Adds form-data parameters as the entire body of the request.
707    *
708    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
709    * <p class='bjava'>
710    *    <jc>// Creates form data "foo=bar&amp;baz=qux".</jc>
711    *    <jv>client</jv>
712    *       .formPost(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
713    *       .formDataCustom(<js>"foo=bar&amp;baz=qux"</js>)
714    *       .run();
715    *
716    *    <jc>// Creates form data "foo=bar&amp;baz=qux" using StringEntity.</jc>
717    *    <jv>client</jv>
718    *       .formPost(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
719    *       .formDataCustom(<jk>new</jk> StringEntity(<js>"foo=bar&amp;baz=qux"</js>,<js>"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"</js>))
720    *       .run();
721    *
722    *    <jc>// Creates form data "foo=bar&amp;baz=qux" using StringEntity and body().</jc>
723    *    <jv>client</jv>
724    *       .formPost(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
725    *       .content(<jk>new</jk> StringEntity(<js>"foo=bar&amp;baz=qux"</js>,<js>"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"</js>))
726    *       .run();
727    * </p>
728    *
729    * @param value The parameter value.
730    *    <br>Can be any of the following types:
731    *    <ul class='spaced-list'>
732    *       <li>
733    *          {@link CharSequence}
734    *       <li>
735    *          {@link Reader} - Raw contents of {@code Reader} will be serialized to remote resource.
736    *       <li>
737    *          {@link InputStream} - Raw contents of {@code InputStream} will be serialized to remote resource.
738    *       <li>
739    *          {@link HttpResource} - Raw contents will be serialized to remote resource.  Additional headers and media type will be set on request.
740    *       <li>
741    *          {@link HttpEntity}/{@link BasicHttpEntity} - Bypass Juneau serialization and pass HttpEntity directly to HttpClient.
742    *       <li>
743    *          {@link Object} - POJO to be converted to text using the {@link Serializer} registered with the
744    *          {@link RestClient}.
745    *       <li>
746    *          {@link PartList} - Converted to a URL-encoded FORM post.
747    *    </ul>
748    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (no body will be sent).
749    * @return This object.
750    */
751   public RestRequest formDataCustom(Object value) {
752      header(ContentType.APPLICATION_FORM_URLENCODED);
753      content(value instanceof CharSequence ? new StringReader(value.toString()) : value);
754      return this;
755   }
756
757   /**
758    * Adds form-data parameters to the request body using free-form key/value pairs.
759    *
760    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
761    * <p class='bjava'>
762    *    <jc>// Creates form data "key1=val1&amp;key2=val2".</jc>
763    *    <jv>client</jv>
764    *       .formPost(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
765    *       .formDataPairs(<js>"key1"</js>,<js>"val1"</js>,<js>"key2"</js>,<js>"val2"</js>)
766    *       .run();
767    * </p>
768    *
769    * @param pairs The form-data key/value pairs.
770    *    <ul>
771    *       <li>Values can be any POJO.
772    *       <li>Values converted to a string using the configured part serializer.
773    *    </ul>
774    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
775    *    <br>Must have an even number of elements (key/value pairs).
776    *    <br>Null keys are ignored (parameter will not be created).
777    *    <br>Null values are allowed.
778    * @return This object.
779    * @throws RestCallException Invalid input.
780    */
781   public RestRequest formDataPairs(String...pairs) throws RestCallException {
782      assertArg(pairs.length % 2 == 0, "Odd number of parameters passed into formDataPairs(String...)");
783      var b = formData;
784      for (var i = 0; i < pairs.length; i += 2)
785         b.append(pairs[i], pairs[i + 1]);
786      return this;
787   }
788
789   /**
790    * Returns all the headers of this message.
791    *
792    * Headers are ordered in the sequence they will be sent over a connection.
793    *
794    * @return All the headers of this message
795    */
796   @Override /* Overridden from HttpMessage */
797   public Header[] getAllHeaders() { return headerData.getAll(); }
798
799   /**
800    * Returns the actual request configuration.
801    *
802    * @return The actual request configuration.
803    */
804   @Override /* Overridden from Configurable */
805   public RequestConfig getConfig() { return request.getConfig(); }
806
807   /**
808    * Returns the first header with a specified name of this message.
809    *
810    * Header values are ignored.
811    * <br>If there is more than one matching header in the message the first element of {@link #getHeaders(String)} is returned.
812    * <br>If there is no matching header in the message <jk>null</jk> is returned.
813    *
814    * @param name The name of the header to return.
815    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
816    * @return The first header whose name property equals name or <jk>null</jk> if no such header could be found.
817    */
818   @Override /* Overridden from HttpMessage */
819   public Header getFirstHeader(String name) {
820      return headerData.getFirst(name).orElse(null);
821   }
822
823   /**
824    * Returns the form data for the request.
825    *
826    * @return An immutable list of form data to send on the request.
827    */
828   public PartList getFormData() { return formData; }
829
830   /**
831    * Returns the header data for the request.
832    *
833    * @return An immutable list of headers to send on the request.
834    */
835   public HeaderList getHeaders() { return headerData; }
836
837   /**
838    * Returns all the headers with a specified name of this message.
839    *
840    * Header values are ignored.
841    * <br>Headers are ordered in the sequence they will be sent over a connection.
842    *
843    * @param name The name of the headers to return.
844    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
845    * @return The headers whose name property equals name.
846    */
847   @Override /* Overridden from HttpMessage */
848   public Header[] getHeaders(String name) {
849      return headerData.getAll(name);
850   }
851
852   /**
853    * Returns the body of this request.
854    *
855    * @return The body of this request, or <jk>null</jk> if it doesn't have a body.
856    */
857   public HttpEntity getHttpEntity() { return hasHttpEntity() ? ((HttpEntityEnclosingRequestBase)request).getEntity() : null; }
858
859   /**
860    * Returns the last header with a specified name of this message.
861    *
862    * Header values are ignored.
863    * <br>If there is more than one matching header in the message the last element of {@link #getHeaders(String)} is returned.
864    * <br>If there is no matching header in the message null is returned.
865    *
866    * @param name The name of the header to return.
867    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
868    * @return The last header whose name property equals name or <jk>null</jk> if no such header could be found.
869    */
870   @Override /* Overridden from HttpMessage */
871   public Header getLastHeader(String name) {
872      return headerData.getLast(name).orElse(null);
873   }
874
875   /**
876    * Returns the HTTP method this request uses, such as GET, PUT, POST, or other.
877    *
878    * @return The HTTP method this request uses, such as GET, PUT, POST, or other.
879    */
880   @Override /* Overridden from HttpUriRequest */
881   public String getMethod() { return request.getMethod(); }
882
883   /**
884    * Returns the parameters effective for this message as set by {@link #setParams(HttpParams)}.
885    *
886    * @return The parameters effective for this message as set by {@link #setParams(HttpParams)}.
887    * @deprecated Use constructor parameters of configuration API provided by HttpClient.
888    */
889   @Override /* Overridden from HttpMessage */
890   @Deprecated(since = "10.0", forRemoval = true)
891   public HttpParams getParams() { return request.getParams(); }
892
893   /**
894    * Returns the path data for the request.
895    *
896    * @return An immutable list of path data to send on the request.
897    */
898   public PartList getPathData() { return pathData; }
899
900   /**
901    * Returns the protocol version this message is compatible with.
902    *
903    * @return The protocol version.
904    */
905   @Override /* Overridden from HttpMessage */
906   public ProtocolVersion getProtocolVersion() { return request.getProtocolVersion(); }
907
908   /**
909    * Returns the query data for the request.
910    *
911    * @return An immutable list of query data to send on the request.
912    */
913   public PartList getQueryData() { return queryData; }
914
915   /**
916    * Returns the request line of this request.
917    *
918    * @return The request line.
919    */
920   @Override /* Overridden from HttpRequest */
921   public RequestLine getRequestLine() { return request.getRequestLine(); }
922
923   /**
924    * A shortcut for calling <c>run().getContent().as(<js>type</js>)</c>.
925    *
926    * @param type The object type to create.
927    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
928    * @param <T> The object type to create.
929    * @see ResponseContent#as(Class)
930    * @return The response content as a simple string.
931    * @throws RestCallException If an exception or non-200 response code occurred during the connection attempt.
932    */
933   public <T> T getResponse(Class<T> type) throws RestCallException {
934      return run().getContent().as(type);
935   }
936
937   /**
938    * A shortcut for calling <c>run().getContent().as(<js>type</js>,<js>args</js>)</c>.
939    *
940    * @param <T>
941    *    The object type to create.
942    * @param type
943    *    The object type to create.
944    *    <br>Can be any of the following: {@link ClassMeta}, {@link Class}, {@link ParameterizedType}, {@link GenericArrayType}
945    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
946    * @param args
947    *    The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
948    *    <br>Can be any of the following: {@link ClassMeta}, {@link Class}, {@link ParameterizedType}, {@link GenericArrayType}
949    *    <br>Ignored if the main type is not a map or collection.
950    *    <br>Can contain <jk>null</jk> values (ignored).
951    * @see ResponseContent#as(Type,Type...)
952    * @return The response content as a simple string.
953    * @throws RestCallException If an exception or non-200 response code occurred during the connection attempt.
954    */
955   public <T> T getResponse(Type type, Type...args) throws RestCallException {
956      return run().getContent().as(type, args);
957   }
958
959   /**
960    * A shortcut for calling <c>run().getContent().asString()</c>.
961    *
962    * @return The response content as a simple string.
963    * @throws RestCallException If an exception or non-200 response code occurred during the connection attempt.
964    */
965   public String getResponseAsString() throws RestCallException { return run().getContent().asString(); }
966
967   /**
968    * Returns the original request URI.
969    *
970    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
971    *    <li class='note'>URI remains unchanged in the course of request execution and is not updated if the request is redirected to another location.
972    * </ul>
973    *
974    * @return The original request URI.
975    */
976   @Override /* Overridden from HttpUriRequest */
977   public URI getURI() { return request.getURI(); }
978
979   /**
980    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this request has a body.
981    *
982    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this request has a body.
983    */
984   public boolean hasHttpEntity() {
985      return request instanceof HttpEntityEnclosingRequestBase;
986   }
987
988   /**
989    * Appends a header to the request.
990    *
991    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
992    * <p class='bjava'>
993    *    <jc>// Adds header "Foo: bar".</jc>
994    *    <jv>client</jv>
995    *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
996    *       .header(Accept.<jsf>TEXT_XML</jsf>)
997    *       .run();
998    * </p>
999    *
1000    * @param part
1001    *    The parameter to set.
1002    *    <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
1003    * @return This object.
1004    */
1005   public RestRequest header(Header part) {
1006      return headers(part);
1007   }
1008
1009   /**
1010    * Appends a header to the request.
1011    *
1012    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1013    * <p class='bjava'>
1014    *    <jc>// Adds header "Foo: bar".</jc>
1015    *    <jv>client</jv>
1016    *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
1017    *       .header(<js>"Foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>)
1018    *       .run();
1019    * </p>
1020    *
1021    * @param name
1022    *    The parameter name.
1023    * @param value
1024    *    The parameter value.
1025    *    <br>Non-string values are converted to strings using the {@link HttpPartSerializer} defined on the client.
1026    * @return This object.
1027    */
1028   public RestRequest header(String name, Object value) {
1029      headerData.append(createHeader(name, value));
1030      return this;
1031   }
1032
1033   /**
1034    * Returns an iterator of all the headers.
1035    *
1036    * @return Iterator that returns {@link Header} objects in the sequence they are sent over a connection.
1037    */
1038   @Override /* Overridden from HttpMessage */
1039   public HeaderIterator headerIterator() {
1040      return headerData.headerIterator();
1041   }
1042
1043   /**
1044    * Returns an iterator of the headers with a given name.
1045    *
1046    * @param name the name of the headers over which to iterate, or <jk>null</jk> for all headers.
1047    * @return Iterator that returns {@link Header} objects with the argument name in the sequence they are sent over a connection.
1048    */
1049   @Override /* Overridden from HttpMessage */
1050   public HeaderIterator headerIterator(String name) {
1051      return headerData.headerIterator(name);
1052   }
1053
1054   /**
1055    * Appends multiple headers to the request using freeform key/value pairs.
1056    *
1057    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1058    * <p class='bjava'>
1059    *    <jc>// Adds headers "Foo: bar" and "Baz: qux".</jc>
1060    *    <jv>client</jv>
1061    *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
1062    *       .headerPairs(<js>"Foo"</js>,<js>"bar"</js>,<js>"Baz"</js>,<js>"qux"</js>)
1063    *       .run();
1064    * </p>
1065    *
1066    * @param pairs The header key/value pairs.
1067    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1068    *    <br>Must have an even number of elements (key/value pairs).
1069    *    <br>Null keys are ignored (header will not be created).
1070    *    <br>Null values are allowed.
1071    * @return This object.
1072    */
1073   public RestRequest headerPairs(String...pairs) {
1074      assertArg(pairs.length % 2 == 0, "Odd number of parameters passed into headerPairs(String...)");
1075      var b = headerData;
1076      for (var i = 0; i < pairs.length; i += 2)
1077         b.append(pairs[i], pairs[i + 1]);
1078      return this;
1079   }
1080
1081   /**
1082    * Appends multiple headers to the request.
1083    *
1084    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1085    * <p class='bjava'>
1086    *    <jc>// Appends two headers to the request.</jc>
1087    *    <jv>client</jv>
1088    *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
1089    *       .headers(
1090    *          BasicHeader.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"Foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>),
1091    *          Accept.<jsf>TEXT_XML</jsf>
1092    *       )
1093    *       .run();
1094    * </p>
1095    *
1096    * @param parts
1097    *    The parameters to set.
1098    *    <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
1099    * @return This object.
1100    */
1101   public RestRequest headers(Header...parts) {
1102      headerData.append(parts);
1103      return this;
1104   }
1105
1106   /**
1107    * Appends multiple headers to the request from properties defined on a Java bean.
1108    *
1109    * <p>
1110    * Uses {@link PropertyNamerDUCS} for resolving the header names from property names.
1111    *
1112    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1113    * <p class='bjava'>
1114    *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>
1115    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyHeaders {
1116    *       <jk>public</jk> String getFooBar() { <jk>return</jk> <js>"baz"</js>; }
1117    *       <jk>public</jk> Integer getQux() { <jk>return</jk> 123; }
1118    *    }
1119    *
1120    *    <jc>// Appends headers "Foo-Bar: baz" and "Qux: 123".</jc>
1121    *    <jv>client</jv>
1122    *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
1123    *       .headersBean(<jk>new</jk> MyHeaders())
1124    *       .run();
1125    * </p>
1126    *
1127    * @param value The bean containing the properties to set as header values.
1128    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1129    * @return This object.
1130    */
1131   public RestRequest headersBean(Object value) {
1132      assertArg(isBean(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value)), "Object passed into headersBean(Object) is not a bean.");
1133      var b = headerData;
1134      toBeanMap(value, PropertyNamerDUCS.INSTANCE).forEach((k, v) -> b.append(createHeader(k, v)));
1135      return this;
1136   }
1137
1138   /**
1139    * Convenience method for specifying HTML as the marshalling transmission media type for this request only.
1140    *
1141    * <p>
1142    * POJOs are converted to HTML without any sort of doc wrappers.
1143    *
1144    * <p>
1145    *    {@link HtmlSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
1146    *    <ul>
1147    *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#sortCollections()}) or
1148    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1149    *    </ul>
1150    * <p>
1151    *    {@link HtmlParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
1152    *    <ul>
1153    *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
1154    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1155    *    </ul>
1156    * <p>
1157    *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/html"</js> unless overridden
1158    *       by {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#accept(String)}.
1159    * <p>
1160    *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/html"</js> unless overridden
1161    *       by {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#contentType(String)}.
1162    * <p>
1163    *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(HtmlSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(HtmlParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
1164    *
1165    * @return This object.
1166    */
1167   public RestRequest html() {
1168      return serializer(HtmlSerializer.class).parser(HtmlParser.class);
1169   }
1170
1171   /**
1172    * Convenience method for specifying HTML DOC as the marshalling transmission media type for this request only.
1173    *
1174    * <p>
1175    * POJOs are converted to fully renderable HTML pages.
1176    *
1177    * <p>
1178    *    {@link HtmlDocSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
1179    *    <ul>
1180    *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#sortCollections()}) or
1181    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1182    *    </ul>
1183    * <p>
1184    *    {@link HtmlParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
1185    *    <ul>
1186    *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
1187    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1188    *    </ul>
1189    * <p>
1190    *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/html"</js> unless overridden
1191    *       by {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#accept(String)}.
1192    * <p>
1193    *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/html"</js> unless overridden
1194    *       by {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#contentType(String)}.
1195    * <p>
1196    *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(HtmlDocSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(HtmlParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
1197    *
1198    * @return This object.
1199    */
1200   public RestRequest htmlDoc() {
1201      return serializer(HtmlDocSerializer.class).parser(HtmlParser.class);
1202   }
1203
1204   /**
1205    * Convenience method for specifying Stripped HTML DOC as the marshalling transmission media type for this request only.
1206    *
1207    * <p>
1208    * Same as {@link #htmlDoc()} but without the header and body tags and page title and description.
1209    *
1210    * <p>
1211    *    {@link HtmlStrippedDocSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
1212    *    <ul>
1213    *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#sortCollections()}) or
1214    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1215    *    </ul>
1216    * <p>
1217    *    {@link HtmlParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
1218    *    <ul>
1219    *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
1220    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1221    *    </ul>
1222    * <p>
1223    *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/html+stripped"</js> unless overridden
1224    *       by {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#accept(String)}.
1225    * <p>
1226    *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/html+stripped"</js> unless overridden
1227    *       by {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#contentType(String)}.
1228    * <p>
1229    *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(HtmlStrippedDocSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(HtmlParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
1230    *
1231    * @return This object.
1232    */
1233   public RestRequest htmlStrippedDoc() {
1234      return serializer(HtmlStrippedDocSerializer.class).parser(HtmlParser.class);
1235   }
1236
1237   /**
1238    * Prevent {@link RestCallException RestCallExceptions} from being thrown when HTTP status 400+ is encountered.
1239    *
1240    * <p>
1241    * This overrides the <l>ignoreErrors</l> property on the client.
1242    *
1243    * @return This object.
1244    */
1245   public RestRequest ignoreErrors() {
1246      this.ignoreErrors = true;
1247      return this;
1248   }
1249
1250   /**
1251    * Add one or more interceptors for this call only.
1252    *
1253    * @param interceptors The interceptors to add to this call.
1254    *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
1255    * @return This object.
1256    * @throws RestCallException If init method on interceptor threw an exception.
1257    */
1258   public RestRequest interceptors(RestCallInterceptor...interceptors) throws RestCallException {
1259      assertArgNoNulls(ARG_interceptors, interceptors);
1260      try {
1261         for (var i : interceptors) {
1262            this.interceptors.add(i);
1263            i.onInit(this);
1264         }
1265      } catch (RuntimeException | RestCallException e) {
1266         throw e;
1267      } catch (Exception e) {
1268         throw new RestCallException(null, e, "Interceptor threw an exception on init.");
1269      }
1270
1271      return this;
1272   }
1273
1274   @Override /* Overridden from HttpUriRequest */
1275   public boolean isAborted() { return request.isAborted(); }
1276
1277   /**
1278    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if debug mode is currently enabled.
1279    */
1280   @Override
1281   public boolean isDebug() { return headerData.get("Debug", Boolean.class).orElse(false); }
1282
1283   /**
1284    * Convenience method for specifying JSON as the marshalling transmission media type for this request only.
1285    *
1286    * <p>
1287    * {@link JsonSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
1288    *    <ul>
1289    *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#sortCollections()}) or
1290    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1291    *    </ul>
1292    * <p>
1293    *    {@link JsonParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
1294    *    <ul>
1295    *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
1296    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1297    *    </ul>
1298    * <p>
1299    *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"application/json"</js> unless overridden
1300    *       by {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#accept(String)}.
1301    * <p>
1302    *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"application/json"</js> unless overridden
1303    *       {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#contentType(String)}.
1304    * <p>
1305    *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(JsonSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(JsonParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
1306    *
1307    * @return This object.
1308    */
1309   public RestRequest json() {
1310      return serializer(JsonSerializer.class).parser(JsonParser.class);
1311   }
1312
1313   /**
1314    * Convenience method for specifying Simplified JSON as the marshalling transmission media type for this request only.
1315    *
1316    * <p>
1317    * Simplified JSON is typically useful for automated tests because you can do simple string comparison of results
1318    * without having to escape lots of quotes.
1319    *
1320    * <p>
1321    *    {@link Json5Serializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
1322    *    <ul>
1323    *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#sortCollections()}) or
1324    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1325    *    </ul>
1326    * <p>
1327    *    {@link Json5Parser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
1328    *    <ul>
1329    *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
1330    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1331    *    </ul>
1332    * <p>
1333    *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"application/json"</js> unless overridden
1334    *       by {@link #header(String,Object)} or {@link #accept(String)}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#accept(String)}.
1335    * <p>
1336    *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"application/json+simple"</js> unless overridden
1337    *       by {@link #header(String,Object)} or {@link #contentType(String)}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#contentType(String)}.
1338    * <p>
1339    *    Can be combined with other marshaller setters such as {@link #xml()} to provide support for multiple languages.
1340    *    <ul>
1341    *       <li>When multiple languages are supported, the <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> headers control which marshallers are used, or uses the
1342    *       last-enabled language if the headers are not set.
1343    *    </ul>
1344    * <p>
1345    *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(Json5Serializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(Json5Parser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
1346    *
1347    * @return This object.
1348    */
1349   public RestRequest json5() {
1350      return serializer(Json5Serializer.class).parser(Json5Parser.class);
1351   }
1352
1353   /**
1354    * Convenience method for specifying JCS (JSON Canonicalization Scheme, RFC 8785) as the marshalling transmission media type for this request only.
1355    *
1356    * <p>
1357    *    {@link JcsSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies.
1358    *    {@link JsonParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies (JCS output is valid JSON).
1359    * <p>
1360    *    <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> will be set to <js>"application/jcs+json"</js>.
1361    * <p>
1362    *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(JcsSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(JsonParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
1363    *
1364    * @return This object.
1365    */
1366   public RestRequest jcs() {
1367      return serializer(JcsSerializer.class).parser(JsonParser.class);
1368   }
1369
1370   /**
1371    * Logs a message.
1372    *
1373    * @param level The log level.
1374    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1375    * @param msg The message with {@link MessageFormat}-style arguments.
1376    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1377    * @param args The arguments.
1378    *    <br>Can contain <jk>null</jk> values.
1379    * @return This object.
1380    */
1381   public RestRequest log(Level level, String msg, Object...args) {
1382      client.log(level, msg, args);
1383      return this;
1384   }
1385
1386   /**
1387    * Logs a message.
1388    *
1389    * @param level The log level.
1390    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1391    * @param t The throwable cause.
1392    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1393    * @param msg The message with {@link MessageFormat}-style arguments.
1394    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1395    * @param args The arguments.
1396    *    <br>Can contain <jk>null</jk> values.
1397    * @return This object.
1398    */
1399   public RestRequest log(Level level, Throwable t, String msg, Object...args) {
1400      client.log(level, t, msg, args);
1401      return this;
1402   }
1403
1404   /**
1405    * Shortcut for setting the <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> headers on a request.
1406    *
1407    * @param value The new header values.
1408    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (headers will not be set).
1409    * @return This object.
1410    * @throws RestCallException Invalid input.
1411    */
1412   public RestRequest mediaType(String value) throws RestCallException {
1413      return header(Accept.of(value)).header(ContentType.of(value));
1414   }
1415
1416   /**
1417    * Convenience method for specifying BSON as the marshalling transmission media type for this request only.
1418    *
1419    * <p>
1420    * Identical to calling <c>serializer(BsonSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(BsonParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
1421    *
1422    * @return This object.
1423    */
1424   public RestRequest bson() {
1425      return serializer(BsonSerializer.class).parser(BsonParser.class);
1426   }
1427
1428   /**
1429    * Convenience method for specifying MessagePack as the marshalling transmission media type for this request only.
1430    *
1431    * <p>
1432    * MessagePack is a binary equivalent to JSON that takes up considerably less space than JSON.
1433    *
1434    * <p>
1435    *    {@link MsgPackSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
1436    *    <ul>
1437    *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#sortCollections()}) or
1438    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1439    *    </ul>
1440    * <p>
1441    *    {@link MsgPackParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
1442    *    <ul>
1443    *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
1444    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1445    *    </ul>
1446    * <p>
1447    *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"application/msgpack"</js> unless overridden
1448    *       by {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#accept(String)}.
1449    * <p>
1450    *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"application/msgpack"</js> unless overridden
1451    *       by {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#contentType(String)}.
1452    * <p>
1453    *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(MsgPackSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(MsgPackParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
1454    *
1455    * @return This object.
1456    */
1457   public RestRequest msgPack() {
1458      return serializer(MsgPackSerializer.class).parser(MsgPackParser.class);
1459   }
1460
1461   /**
1462    * When called, <c>No-Trace: true</c> is added to requests.
1463    *
1464    * <p>
1465    * This gives the opportunity for the servlet to not log errors on invalid requests.
1466    * This is useful for testing purposes when you don't want your log file to show lots of errors that are simply the
1467    * results of testing.
1468    *
1469    * @return This object.
1470    * @throws RestCallException Invalid input.
1471    */
1472   public RestRequest noTrace() throws RestCallException {
1473      return header(NoTrace.TRUE);
1474   }
1475
1476   /**
1477    * Convenience method for specifying OpenAPI as the marshalling transmission media type for this request only.
1478    *
1479    * <p>
1480    * OpenAPI is a language that allows serialization to formats that use {@link HttpPartSchema} objects to describe their structure.
1481    *
1482    * <p>
1483    *    {@link OpenApiSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
1484    *    <ul>
1485    *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#sortCollections()}) or
1486    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1487    *       <li>Typically the {@link RestRequest#content(Object, HttpPartSchema)} method will be used to specify the body of the request with the
1488    *          schema describing it's structure.
1489    *    </ul>
1490    * <p>
1491    *    {@link OpenApiParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
1492    *    <ul>
1493    *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
1494    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1495    *       <li>Typically the {@link ResponseContent#schema(HttpPartSchema)} method will be used to specify the structure of the response body.
1496    *    </ul>
1497    * <p>
1498    *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/openapi"</js> unless overridden
1499    *       by {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#accept(String)}.
1500    * <p>
1501    *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/openapi"</js> unless overridden
1502    *       by {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#contentType(String)}.
1503    * <p>
1504    *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(OpenApiSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(OpenApiParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
1505    *
1506    * @return This object.
1507    */
1508   public RestRequest openApi() {
1509      return serializer(OpenApiSerializer.class).parser(OpenApiParser.class);
1510   }
1511
1512   /**
1513    * Specifies the parser to use on the response body.
1514    *
1515    * <p>
1516    * Overrides the parsers specified on the {@link RestClient}.
1517    *
1518    * <p>
1519    *    The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
1520    *    bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1521    *
1522    * <p>
1523    * If the <c>Accept</c> header is not set on the request, it will be set to the media type of this parser.
1524    *
1525    * @param parser The parser used to parse POJOs from the body of the HTTP response.
1526    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1527    * @return This object.
1528    */
1529   public RestRequest parser(Class<? extends Parser> parser) {
1530      this.parser = client.getInstance(assertArgNotNull(ARG_parser, parser));
1531      return this;
1532   }
1533
1534   /**
1535    * Specifies the parser to use on the response body.
1536    *
1537    * <p>
1538    * Overrides the parsers specified on the {@link RestClient}.
1539    *
1540    * <p>
1541    *    The parser is not modified by any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
1542    *    bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1543    *
1544    * <p>
1545    * If the <c>Accept</c> header is not set on the request, it will be set to the media type of this parser.
1546    *
1547    * @param parser The parser used to parse POJOs from the body of the HTTP response.
1548    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1549    * @return This object.
1550    */
1551   public RestRequest parser(Parser parser) {
1552      this.parser = assertArgNotNull(ARG_parser, parser);
1553      return this;
1554   }
1555
1556   /**
1557    * Sets or replaces multiple path parameters on the request.
1558    *
1559    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1560    * <p class='bjava'>
1561    *    <jc>// Appends two path parameters to the request.</jc>
1562    *    <jv>client</jv>
1563    *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
1564    *       .pathData(
1565    *          BasicStringPart.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>),
1566    *          BasicBooleanPart.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"baz"</js>, <jk>true</jk>)
1567    *       )
1568    *       .run();
1569    * </p>
1570    *
1571    * @param parts
1572    *    The parameters to set.
1573    *    <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
1574    * @return This object.
1575    */
1576   public RestRequest pathData(NameValuePair...parts) {
1577      pathData.set(parts);
1578      return this;
1579   }
1580
1581   /**
1582    * Sets or replaces a path parameter of the form <js>"{name}"</js> in the URI.
1583    *
1584    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1585    * <p class='bjava'>
1586    *    <jc>// Sets path to "/bar".</jc>
1587    *    <jv>client</jv>
1588    *       .get(<js>"/{foo}"</js>)
1589    *       .pathData(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>)
1590    *       .run();
1591    * </p>
1592    *
1593    * @param name
1594    *    The parameter name.
1595    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk> or empty (parameter will not be created).
1596    * @param value
1597    *    The parameter value.
1598    *    <br>Non-string values are converted to strings using the {@link HttpPartSerializer} defined on the client.
1599    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1600    * @return This object.
1601    */
1602   public RestRequest pathData(String name, Object value) {
1603      pathData.set(createPart(PATH, name, value));
1604      return this;
1605   }
1606
1607   /**
1608    * Sets multiple path parameters to the request from properties defined on a Java bean.
1609    *
1610    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1611    * <p class='bjava'>
1612    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyPathVars {
1613    *       <jk>public</jk> String getFooBar() { <jk>return</jk> <js>"baz"</js>; }
1614    *       <jk>public</jk> Integer getQux() { <jk>return</jk> 123; }
1615    *    }
1616    *
1617    *    <jc>// Given path "/{fooBar}/{qux}/", gets converted to "/baz/123/".</jc>
1618    *    <jv>client</jv>
1619    *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
1620    *       .pathDataBean(<jk>new</jk> MyPathVars())
1621    *       .run();
1622    * </p>
1623    *
1624    * @param value The bean containing the properties to set as path parameter values.
1625    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1626    * @return This object.
1627    */
1628   public RestRequest pathDataBean(Object value) {
1629      assertArg(isBean(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value)), "Object passed into pathDataBean(Object) is not a bean.");
1630      var b = pathData;
1631      toBeanMap(value).forEach((k, v) -> b.set(createPart(PATH, k, v)));
1632      return this;
1633   }
1634
1635   /**
1636    * Replaces path parameters of the form <js>"{name}"</js> in the URI using free-form key/value pairs.
1637    *
1638    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1639    * <p class='bjava'>
1640    *    <jc>// Sets path to "/baz/qux".</jc>
1641    *    <jv>client</jv>
1642    *       .get(<js>"/{foo}/{bar}"</js>)
1643    *       .pathDataPairs(
1644    *          <js>"foo"</js>,<js>"baz"</js>,
1645    *          <js>"bar"</js>,<js>"qux"</js>
1646    *       )
1647    *       .run();
1648    * </p>
1649    *
1650    * @param pairs The path key/value pairs.
1651    *    <ul>
1652    *       <li>Values can be any POJO.
1653    *       <li>Values converted to a string using the configured part serializer.
1654    *    </ul>
1655    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1656    *    <br>Must have an even number of elements (key/value pairs).
1657    *    <br>Null keys are ignored (parameter will not be created).
1658    *    <br>Null values are allowed.
1659    * @return This object.
1660    */
1661   public RestRequest pathDataPairs(String...pairs) {
1662      assertArg(pairs.length % 2 == 0, "Odd number of parameters passed into pathDataPairs(String...)");
1663      var b = pathData;
1664      for (var i = 0; i < pairs.length; i += 2)
1665         b.set(pairs[i], pairs[i + 1]);
1666      return this;
1667   }
1668
1669   /**
1670    * Convenience method for specifying Plain Text as the marshalling transmission media type for this request only.
1671    *
1672    * <p>
1673    * Plain text marshalling typically only works on simple POJOs that can be converted to and from strings using
1674    * swaps, swap methods, etc...
1675    *
1676    * <p>
1677    *    {@link PlainTextSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
1678    *    <ul>
1679    *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#sortCollections()}) or
1680    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1681    *    </ul>
1682    * <p>
1683    *    {@link PlainTextParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
1684    *    <ul>
1685    *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
1686    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
1687    *    </ul>
1688    * <p>
1689    *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/plain"</js> unless overridden
1690    *       by {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#accept(String)}.
1691    * <p>
1692    *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/plain"</js> unless overridden
1693    *       by {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#contentType(String)}.
1694    * <p>
1695    *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(PlainTextSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(PlainTextParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
1696    *
1697    * @return This object.
1698    */
1699   public RestRequest plainText() {
1700      return serializer(PlainTextSerializer.class).parser(PlainTextParser.class);
1701   }
1702
1703   /**
1704    * Sets the {@code X-Juneau-Serializer-Options} header for this request.
1705    *
1706    * <p>
1707    * The value is a JSON5 object whose keys are serializer session property names and values are the overrides.
1708    * Example: <js>"{escapeSolidus:true,maxIndent:4}"</js>.
1709    *
1710    * @param json5
1711    *    Serializer session options as JSON5. Can be <jk>null</jk> (header is not set).
1712    * @return This object.
1713    */
1714   public RestRequest serializerSessionOptionsHeader(String json5) {
1715      return header(HEADER_JuneauSerializerOptions, json5);
1716   }
1717
1718   /**
1719    * Sets the {@code X-Juneau-Parser-Options} header for this request.
1720    *
1721    * <p>
1722    * The value is a JSON5 object whose keys are parser session property names and values are the overrides.
1723    *
1724    * @param json5
1725    *    Parser session options as JSON5. Can be <jk>null</jk> (header is not set).
1726    * @return This object.
1727    */
1728   public RestRequest parserSessionOptionsHeader(String json5) {
1729      return header(HEADER_JuneauParserOptions, json5);
1730   }
1731
1732   /**
1733    * Convenience for {@link #serializerSessionOptionsHeader(String)} using a map serialized with {@link Json5#of(Object)}.
1734    *
1735    * @param properties
1736    *    Property map for serializer session options. Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as empty).
1737    * @return This object.
1738    * @throws SerializeException If serializing {@code properties} to JSON5 fails.
1739    */
1740   public RestRequest serializerSessionOptionsHeader(Map<String,?> properties) {
1741      if (isEmpty(properties))
1742         return this;
1743      return header(HEADER_JuneauSerializerOptions, Json5.of(properties));
1744   }
1745
1746   /**
1747    * Convenience for {@link #parserSessionOptionsHeader(String)} using a map serialized with {@link Json5#of(Object)}.
1748    *
1749    * @param properties
1750    *    Property map for parser session options. Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as empty).
1751    * @return This object.
1752    * @throws SerializeException If serializing {@code properties} to JSON5 fails.
1753    */
1754   public RestRequest parserSessionOptionsHeader(Map<String,?> properties) {
1755      if (isEmpty(properties))
1756         return this;
1757      return header(HEADER_JuneauParserOptions, Json5.of(properties));
1758   }
1759
1760   /**
1761    * Sets the {@code juneauSerializerOptions} query parameter for this request (UON-encoded map).
1762    *
1763    * <p>
1764    * Example: <js>"(escapeSolidus=true,maxIndent=4)"</js>.
1765    *
1766    * @param uon
1767    *    Serializer session options in UON form. Can be <jk>null</jk> (parameter is not set).
1768    * @return This object.
1769    */
1770   public RestRequest serializerSessionOptionsQuery(String uon) {
1771      return queryData(QUERY_juneauSerializerOptions, uon);
1772   }
1773
1774   /**
1775    * Sets the {@code juneauParserOptions} query parameter for this request (UON-encoded map).
1776    *
1777    * @param uon
1778    *    Parser session options in UON form. Can be <jk>null</jk> (parameter is not set).
1779    * @return This object.
1780    */
1781   public RestRequest parserSessionOptionsQuery(String uon) {
1782      return queryData(QUERY_juneauParserOptions, uon);
1783   }
1784
1785   /**
1786    * Convenience for {@link #serializerSessionOptionsQuery(String)} using {@link Uon#of(Object)}.
1787    *
1788    * @param properties
1789    *    Property map for serializer session options. Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as empty).
1790    * @return This object.
1791    * @throws SerializeException If serializing {@code properties} to UON fails.
1792    */
1793   public RestRequest serializerSessionOptionsQuery(Map<String,?> properties) {
1794      if (isEmpty(properties))
1795         return this;
1796      return serializerSessionOptionsQuery(Uon.of(properties));
1797   }
1798
1799   /**
1800    * Convenience for {@link #parserSessionOptionsQuery(String)} using {@link Uon#of(Object)}.
1801    *
1802    * @param properties
1803    *    Property map for parser session options. Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as empty).
1804    * @return This object.
1805    * @throws SerializeException If serializing {@code properties} to UON fails.
1806    */
1807   public RestRequest parserSessionOptionsQuery(Map<String,?> properties) {
1808      if (isEmpty(properties))
1809         return this;
1810      return parserSessionOptionsQuery(Uon.of(properties));
1811   }
1812
1813   /**
1814    * Sets the protocol version for this request.
1815    *
1816    * @param version The protocol version for this request.
1817    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (default protocol version will be used).
1818    * @return This object.
1819    */
1820   public RestRequest protocolVersion(ProtocolVersion version) {
1821      request.setProtocolVersion(version);
1822      return this;
1823   }
1824
1825   /**
1826    * Adds a free-form custom query.
1827    *
1828    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1829    * <p class='bjava'>
1830    *    <jc>// Adds query parameter "foo=bar&amp;baz=qux".</jc>
1831    *    <jv>client</jv>
1832    *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
1833    *       .queryCustom(<js>"foo=bar&amp;baz=qux"</js>)
1834    *       .run();
1835    * </p>
1836    *
1837    * @param value The parameter value.
1838    *    <br>Can be any of the following types:
1839    *    <ul>
1840    *       <li>
1841    *          {@link CharSequence}
1842    *       <li>
1843    *          {@link Reader} - Raw contents of {@code Reader} will be serialized to remote resource.
1844    *       <li>
1845    *          {@link InputStream} - Raw contents of {@code InputStream} will be serialized to remote resource.
1846    *       <li>
1847    *          {@link PartList} - Converted to a URL-encoded query.
1848    *    </ul>
1849    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (no custom query will be set).
1850    * @return This object.
1851    */
1852   public RestRequest queryCustom(Object value) {
1853      try {
1854         String q = null;
1855         if (value instanceof Reader value2)
1856            q = read(value2);
1857         else if (value instanceof InputStream value2)
1858            q = read(value2);
1859         else
1860            q = s(value);  // Works for NameValuePairs.
1861         uriBuilder.setCustomQuery(q);
1862      } catch (IOException e) {
1863         throw rex(e, "Could not read custom query.");
1864      }
1865      return this;
1866   }
1867
1868   /**
1869    * Appends multiple query parameters to the request.
1870    *
1871    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1872    * <p class='bjava'>
1873    *    <jc>// Appends two query parameters to the request.</jc>
1874    *    <jv>client</jv>
1875    *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
