1876    *       .queryData(
1877    *          BasicStringPart.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>),
1878    *          BasicBooleanPart.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"baz"</js>, <jk>true</jk>)
1879    *       )
1880    *       .run();
1881    * </p>
1882    *
1883    * @param parts
1884    *    The parameters to set.
1885    *    <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
1886    * @return This object.
1887    */
1888   public RestRequest queryData(NameValuePair...parts) {
1889      queryData.append(parts);
1890      return this;
1891   }
1892
1893   /**
1894    * Appends a query parameter to the URI of the request.
1895    *
1896    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1897    * <p class='bjava'>
1898    *    <jc>// Adds query parameter "foo=bar".</jc>
1899    *    <jv>client</jv>
1900    *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
1901    *       .queryData(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>)
1902    *       .run();
1903    * </p>
1904    *
1905    * @param name
1906    *    The parameter name.
1907    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk> or empty (parameter will not be created).
1908    * @param value
1909    *    The parameter value.
1910    *    <br>Non-string values are converted to strings using the {@link HttpPartSerializer} defined on the client.
1911    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1912    * @return This object.
1913    */
1914   public RestRequest queryData(String name, Object value) {
1915      queryData.append(createPart(QUERY, name, value));
1916      return this;
1917   }
1918
1919   /**
1920    * Appends multiple query parameters to the request from properties defined on a Java bean.
1921    *
1922    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1923    * <p class='bjava'>
1924    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyQuery {
1925    *       <jk>public</jk> String getFooBar() { <jk>return</jk> <js>"baz"</js>; }
1926    *       <jk>public</jk> Integer getQux() { <jk>return</jk> 123; }
1927    *    }
1928    *
1929    *    <jc>// Appends query "fooBar=baz&amp;qux=123".</jc>
1930    *    <jv>client</jv>
1931    *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
1932    *       .queryDataBean(<jk>new</jk> MyQuery())
1933    *       .run();
1934    * </p>
1935    *
1936    * @param value The bean containing the properties to set as query parameter values.
1937    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1938    * @return This object.
1939    */
1940   public RestRequest queryDataBean(Object value) {
1941      assertArg(isBean(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value)), "Object passed into queryDataBean(Object) is not a bean.");
1942      var b = queryData;
1943      toBeanMap(value).forEach((k, v) -> b.append(createPart(QUERY, k, v)));
1944      return this;
1945   }
1946
1947   /**
1948    * Adds query parameters to the URI query using free-form key/value pairs..
1949    *
1950    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1951    * <p class='bjava'>
1952    *    <jc>// Adds query parameters "foo=bar&amp;baz=qux".</jc>
1953    *    <jv>client</jv>
1954    *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
1955    *       .queryDataPairs(<js>"foo"</js>,<js>"bar"</js>,<js>"baz"</js>,<js>"qux"</js>)
1956    *       .run();
1957    * </p>
1958    *
1959    * @param pairs The query key/value pairs.
1960    *    <ul>
1961    *       <li>Values can be any POJO.
1962    *       <li>Values converted to a string using the configured part serializer.
1963    *    </ul>
1964    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1965    *    <br>Must have an even number of elements (key/value pairs).
1966    *    <br>Null keys are ignored (parameter will not be created).
1967    *    <br>Null values are allowed.
1968    * @return This object.
1969    * @throws RestCallException Invalid input.
1970    */
1971   public RestRequest queryDataPairs(String...pairs) throws RestCallException {
1972      assertArg(pairs.length % 2 == 0, "Odd number of parameters passed into queryDataPairs(String...)");
1973      var b = queryData;
1974      for (var i = 0; i < pairs.length; i += 2)
1975         b.append(pairs[i], pairs[i + 1]);
1976      return this;
1977   }
1978
1979   /**
1980    * Removes a header from this message.
1981    *
1982    * @param header The header to remove.
1983    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (no-op).
1984    */
1985   @Override /* Overridden from HttpMessage */
1986   public void removeHeader(Header header) {
1987      headerData.remove(header);
1988   }
1989
1990   /**
1991    * Removes all headers with a certain name from this message.
1992    *
1993    * @param name The name of the headers to remove.
1994    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1995    */
1996   @Override /* Overridden from HttpMessage */
1997   public void removeHeaders(String name) {
1998      headerData.remove(name);
1999   }
2000
2001   /**
2002    * Rethrow any of the specified exception types if a matching <c>Exception-Name</c> header is found.
2003    *
2004    * <p>
2005    * Rethrown exceptions will be set on the caused-by exception of {@link RestCallException} when
2006    * thrown from the {@link #run()} method.
2007    *
2008    * <p>
2009    * Can be called multiple times to append multiple rethrows.
2010    *
2011    * @param values The classes to rethrow.
2012    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2013    *    <br>Null elements are ignored (only non-null classes that extend {@link Throwable} are added).
2014    * @return This object.
2015    */
2016   @SuppressWarnings({
2017      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
2018   })
2019   public RestRequest rethrow(Class<?>...values) {
2020      if (rethrow == null)
2021         rethrow = list();
2022      for (var v : values) {
2023         if (nn(v) && Throwable.class.isAssignableFrom(v))
2024            rethrow.add((Class<? extends Throwable>)v);
2025      }
2026      return this;
2027   }
2028
2029   /**
2030    * Runs this request and returns the resulting response object.
2031    *
2032    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2033    * <p class='bjava'>
2034    *    <jk>try</jk> {
2035    *       <jk>int</jk> <jv>rc</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<jsf>URI</jsf>).execute().getResponseStatus();
2036    *       <jc>// Succeeded!</jc>
2037    *    } <jk>catch</jk> (RestCallException <jv>e</jv>) {
2038    *       <jc>// Failed!</jc>
2039    *    }
2040    * </p>
2041    *
2042    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
2043    *    <li class='note'>Calling this method multiple times will return the same original response object.
2044    *    <li class='note'>You must close the returned object if you do not consume the response or execute a method that consumes
2045    *       the response.
2046    *    <li class='note'>If you are only interested in the response code, use the {@link #complete()} method which will automatically
2047    *       consume the response so that you don't need to call {@link InputStream#close()} on the response body.
2048    * </ul>
2049    *
2050    * @return The response object.
2051    * @throws RestCallException If an exception or non-200 response code occurred during the connection attempt.
2052    */
2053   @SuppressWarnings({
2054      "null", // Null handling verified by context or framework
2055      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for this specific logic
2056      "java:S6541", // Single-threaded context; synchronization unnecessary
2057   })
2058   public RestResponse run() throws RestCallException {
2059      if (nn(response))
2060         throw new RestCallException(response, null, "run() already called.");
2061
2062      try {
2063
2064         queryData.stream().map(SimpleQuery::new).filter(SimplePart::isValid).forEach(x -> uriBuilder.addParameter(x.name, x.value));
2065
2066         pathData.stream().map(SimplePath::new).forEach(x -> {
2067            var path = uriBuilder.getPath();
2068            var name = x.name;
2069            var value = x.value;
2070            var pathVar = "{" + name + "}";
2071            if (path.indexOf(pathVar) == -1 && ! name.equals("/*"))
2072               throw new IllegalStateException("Path variable {" + name + "} was not found in path.");
2073            if (name.equals("/*"))
2074               path = path.replaceAll("\\/\\*$", URI_PATH_SEPARATOR + value);
2075            else
2076               path = path.replace(pathVar, String.valueOf(value));
2077            uriBuilder.setPath(path);
2078         });
2079
2080         HttpEntityEnclosingRequestBase request2 = request instanceof HttpEntityEnclosingRequestBase request3 ? request3 : null;
2081         request.setURI(uriBuilder.build());
2082
2083         // Pick the serializer if it hasn't been overridden.
2084         var hl = headerData;
2085         var h = hl.getLast("Content-Type");
2086         var contentType = h.isPresent() ? h.get().getValue() : null;
2087         var serializer2 = this.serializer;
2088         if (serializer2 == null)
2089            serializer2 = client.getMatchingSerializer(contentType);
2090         if (contentType == null && nn(serializer2))
2091            contentType = serializer2.getPrimaryMediaType().toString();
2092
2093         // Pick the parser if it hasn't been overridden.
2094         h = hl.getLast("Accept");
2095         var accept = h.isPresent() ? h.get().getValue() : null;
2096         var parser2 = this.parser;
2097         if (parser2 == null)
2098            parser2 = client.getMatchingParser(accept);
2099         if (accept == null && nn(parser2))
2100            hl.set(Accept.of(parser2.getPrimaryMediaType()));
2101
2102         headerData.stream().map(SimpleHeader::new).filter(SimplePart::isValid).forEach(request::addHeader);
2103
2104         if (request2 == null && content != NO_BODY)
2105            throw new RestCallException(null, null, "Method does not support content entity.  Method={0}, URI={1}", getMethod(), getURI());
2106
2107         if (nn(request2)) {
2108
2109            Object input2 = null;
2110            if (content != NO_BODY) {
2111               input2 = content;
2112            } else {
2113               input2 = new UrlEncodedFormEntity(formData.stream().map(SimpleFormData::new).filter(SimplePart::isValid).toList());
2114            }
2115
2116            if (input2 instanceof Supplier<?> s)
2117               input2 = s.get();
2118
2119            HttpEntity entity = null;
2120            if (input2 instanceof PartList input3)
2121               entity = new UrlEncodedFormEntity(input3.stream().map(SimpleFormData::new).filter(SimplePart::isValid).toList());
2122            else if (input2 instanceof HttpResource input3) {
2123               input3.getHeaders().forEach(request::addHeader);
2124               entity = (HttpEntity)input2;
2125            } else if (input2 instanceof HttpEntity input3) {
2126               if (input3 instanceof SerializedEntity input4) {
2127                  entity = input4.copyWith(serializer2, contentSchema);
2128               } else {
2129                  entity = input3;
2130               }
2131            } else if (input2 instanceof Reader input3)
2132               entity = readerEntity(input3, getRequestContentType(TEXT_PLAIN));
2133            else if (input2 instanceof InputStream input3)
2134               entity = streamEntity(input3, -1, getRequestContentType(ContentType.APPLICATION_OCTET_STREAM));
2135            else if (input2 instanceof RecordStreamBody body)
2136               entity = streamBodyEntity(body, serializer2);
2137            else if (nn(serializer2))
2138               entity = serializedEntity(input2, serializer2, contentSchema).setContentType(contentType);
2139            else {
2140               if (client.hasSerializers()) {
2141                  if (contentType == null)
2142                     throw new RestCallException(null, null,
2143                        "Content-Type not specified on request.  Cannot match correct serializer.  Use contentType(String) or mediaType(String) to specify transport language.");
2144                  throw new RestCallException(null, null, "No matching serializer for media type ''{0}''", contentType);
2145               }
2146               entity = stringEntity(input2 == null ? "" : MarshallingContext.DEFAULT.getClassMetaForObject(input2).toString(input2), getRequestContentType(TEXT_PLAIN));
2147            }
2148
2149            request2.setEntity(entity);
2150         }
2151
2152         response = client.createResponse(this, client.run(target, request, context), parser2);
2153
2154         if (isDebug() || client.logRequests == DetailLevel.FULL)
2155            response.cacheContent();
2156
2157         for (var rci : interceptors)
2158            rci.onConnect(this, response);
2159         client.onCallConnect(this, response);
2160
2161         var method = getMethod();
2162         var sc = response.getStatusCode();
2163
2164         var thrown = response.getHeader("Thrown").asHeader(Thrown.class);
2165         if (thrown.isPresent() && nn(rethrow)) {
2166            var partsOpt = thrown.asParts();
2167            if (!partsOpt.isPresent() || partsOpt.get().isEmpty())
2168               return response;
2169            var thrownPart = partsOpt.get().get(0);
2170            var className = thrownPart.getClassName();
2171            var message = thrownPart.getMessage();
2172            for (var t : rethrow) {
2173               if (t.getName().equals(className)) {
2174                  ConstructorInfo c = null;
2175                  var ci = ClassInfo.of(t);
2176                  Throwable thrownInstance = null;
2177                  c = ci.getPublicConstructor(x -> x.hasParameterTypes(HttpResponse.class)).orElse(null);
2178                  if (nn(c)) {
2179                     thrownInstance = c.<Throwable>newInstance(response);
2180                  } else {
2181                     c = ci.getPublicConstructor(x -> x.hasParameterTypes(String.class)).orElse(null);
2182                     if (nn(c)) {
2183                        thrownInstance = c.<Throwable>newInstance(nn(message) ? message : response.getContent().asString());
2184                     } else {
2185                        c = ci.getPublicConstructor(x -> x.hasParameterTypes(String.class, Throwable.class)).orElse(null);
2186                        if (nn(c)) {
2187                           thrownInstance = c.<Throwable>newInstance(nn(message) ? message : response.getContent().asString(), null);
2188                        } else {
2189                           c = ci.getPublicConstructor(cons -> cons.getParameterCount() == 0).orElse(null);
2190                           if (nn(c)) {
2191                              thrownInstance = c.<Throwable>newInstance();
2192                           }
2193                        }
2194                     }
2195                  }
2196                  if (nn(thrownInstance)) {
2197                     if (thrownInstance instanceof Exception ex)
2198                        throw ex;
2199                     throw new RestCallException(response, thrownInstance, "Server threw non-Exception: {0}", className);
2200                  }
2201               }
2202            }
2203         }
2204
2205         if (errorCodes.test(sc) && ! ignoreErrors) {
2206            throw new RestCallException(response, null, "HTTP method ''{0}'' call to ''{1}'' caused response code ''{2}, {3}''.\nResponse: \n{4}", method, getURI(), sc, response.getReasonPhrase(),
2207               response.getContent().asAbbreviatedString(1000));
2208         }
2209
2210      } catch (RuntimeException | RestCallException e) {
2211         if (nn(response))
2212            response.close();
2213         throw e;
2214      } catch (Exception e) {
2215         if (nn(response))
2216            response.close();
2217         throw new RestCallException(response, e, "Call failed.");
2218      }
2219
2220      return this.response;
2221   }
2222
2223   /**
2224    * Same as {@link #run()} but allows you to run the call asynchronously.
2225    *
2226    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2227    * <p class='bjava'>
2228    *    Future&lt;RestResponse&gt; <jv>future</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<jsf>URI</jsf>).runFuture();
2229    *
2230    *    <jc>// Do some other stuff</jc>
2231    *
2232    *    <jk>try</jk> {
2233    *       String <jv>body</jv> = <jv>future</jv>.get().getContent().asString();
2234    *       <jc>// Succeeded!</jc>
2235    *    } <jk>catch</jk> (RestCallException <jv>e</jv>) {
2236    *       <jc>// Failed!</jc>
2237    *    }
2238    * </p>
2239    *
2240    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
2241    *    <li class='note'>Use the {@link RestClient.Builder#executorService(ExecutorService, boolean)} method to customize the
2242    *       executor service used for creating {@link Future Futures}.
2243    * </ul>
2244    *
2245    * @return The HTTP status code.
2246    * @throws RestCallException If the executor service was not defined.
2247    */
2248   public Future<RestResponse> runFuture() throws RestCallException {
2249      return client.getExecutorService().submit(this::run);
2250   }
2251
2252   /**
2253    * Specifies the serializer to use on the request body.
2254    *
2255    * <p>
2256    * Overrides the serializers specified on the {@link RestClient}.
2257    *
2258    * <p>
2259    *    The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#sortCollections()}) or
2260    *    bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
2261    *
2262    * <p>
2263    * If the <c>Content-Type</c> header is not set on the request, it will be set to the media type of this serializer.
2264    *
2265    * @param serializer The serializer used to serialize POJOs to the body of the HTTP request.
2266    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will use default serializer matching Content-Type).
2267    * @return This object.
2268    */
2269   public RestRequest serializer(Class<? extends Serializer> serializer) {
2270      this.serializer = serializer == null ? null : client.getInstance(serializer);
2271      return this;
2272   }

2274   /**

2282    *    bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.

2288    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will use default serializer matching Content-Type).
2289    * @return This object.

2291   public RestRequest serializer(Serializer serializer) {
2292      this.serializer = serializer;
2293      return this;

2296   /**
2297    * Overwrites the first header with the same name.
2298    *
2299    * The new header will be appended to the end of the list, if no header with the given name can be found.
2300    *
2301    * @param header The header to set.
2302    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (ignored).
2303    */
2304   @Override /* Overridden from HttpMessage */

2313    *

2315    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk> or empty (header will not be created).
2316    * @param value The value of the header.
2317    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
2318    */
2319   @Override /* Overridden from HttpMessage */
2320   public void setHeader(String name, String value) {
2321      headerData.set(stringHeader(name, value));
2322   }

2332   public void setHeaders(Header[] headers) {
2333      headerData.set(headers);

2375   /**
2376    * Convenience method for specifying UON as the marshalling transmission media type for this request only.
2377    *
2378    * <p>
2379    * UON is Url-Encoding Object notation that is equivalent to JSON but suitable for transmission as URL-encoded
2380    * query and form post values.
2381    *
2382    * <p>
2383    *    {@link UonSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
2384    *    <ul>

2386    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
2387    *    </ul>
2388    * <p>
2389    *    {@link UonParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
2390    *    <ul>
2391    *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
2392    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
2393    *    </ul>
2394    * <p>
2395    *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/uon"</js> unless overridden
2396    *       by {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#accept(String)}.
2397    * <p>
2398    *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/uon"</js> unless overridden
2399    *       by {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#contentType(String)}.
2400    * <p>
2401    *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(UonSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(UonParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
2402    *
2403    * @return This object.
2404    */
2405   public RestRequest uon() {
2406      return serializer(UonSerializer.class).parser(UonParser.class);
2407   }

2409   /**
2410    * Sets the URI for this request.
2411    *
2412    * <p>
2413    * Can be any of the following types:
2414    * <ul>
2415    *    <li>{@link URI}
2416    *    <li>{@link URL}
2417    *    <li>{@link URIBuilder}
2418    *    <li>Anything else converted to a string using {@link Object#toString()}.
2419    * </ul>
2420    *
2421    * <p>
2422    * Relative URI strings will be interpreted as relative to the root URI defined on the client.
2423    *
2424    * @param uri
2425    *    The URI of the remote REST resource.
2426    *    <br>This overrides the URI passed in from the client.
2427    *    <br>Can be any of the following types:
2428    *    <ul>
2429    *       <li>{@link URIBuilder}
2430    *       <li>{@link URI}
2431    *       <li>{@link URL}
2432    *       <li>{@link String}
2433    *       <li>{@link Object} - Converted to <c>String</c> using <c>toString()</c>
2434    *    </ul>
2435    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2436    * @return This object.
2437    * @throws RestCallException Invalid URI syntax detected.
2438    */
2439   public RestRequest uri(Object uri) throws RestCallException {
2440      var x = client.toUri(uri, null);
2441      if (nn(x.getScheme()))
2442         uriBuilder.setScheme(x.getScheme());
2443      if (nn(x.getHost()))
2444         uriBuilder.setHost(x.getHost());
2445      if (x.getPort() != -1)
2446         uriBuilder.setPort(x.getPort());
2447      if (nn(x.getUserInfo()))
2448         uriBuilder.setUserInfo(x.getUserInfo());
2449      if (nn(x.getFragment()))
2450         uriBuilder.setFragment(x.getFragment());
2451      if (nn(x.getQuery()))
2452         uriBuilder.setCustomQuery(x.getQuery());
2453      uriBuilder.setPath(x.getPath());
2454      return this;
2455   }
2456

2539    *          the request body.  Instead, the part serializer specified via {@link RestClient.Builder#partSerializer(Class)} is used.

2545    *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link RestClient.Builder#swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.

2551    *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"</js> unless overridden
2552    *       by {@link RestRequest#header(String,Object)} or {@link RestRequest#contentType(String)}.

2554    *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(UrlEncodingSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(UrlEncodingParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
2555    *
2556    * @return This object.

2592   private Header createHeader(String name, Object value) {
2593      return createHeader(name, value, null, null, null);

2595
2596   private NameValuePair createPart(HttpPartType type, String name, Object value) {
2597      return createPart(name, value, type, null, null, null);
2598   }

2600   private HttpPartSerializerSession getPartSerializerSession(HttpPartSerializer serializer) {
2601      if (serializer == null)
2602         serializer = client.getPartSerializer();
2603      return partSerializerSessions.computeIfAbsent(serializer, HttpPartSerializer::getPartSession);
2604   }
2605
2606   private ContentType getRequestContentType(ContentType def) {
2607      var h = request.getFirstHeader("Content-Type");
2608      if (nn(h)) {
2609         var s = h.getValue();
2610         if (ine(s))
2611            return ContentType.of(s);
2612      }
2613      return def;
2614   }
2615
2616   /**
2617    * Creates a new header.
2618    *
2619    * @param name The header name.
2620    * @param value The header value.
2621    * @param serializer The part serializer to use, or <jk>null</jk> to use the part serializer defined on the client.
2622    * @param schema Optional HTTP part schema to provide to the part serializer.
2623    * @param skipIfEmpty If <jk>true</jk>, empty string values will be ignored on the request.
2624    * @return A new header.
2625    */
2626   protected Header createHeader(String name, Object value, HttpPartSerializer serializer, HttpPartSchema schema, Boolean skipIfEmpty) {
2627      if (ie(name))
2628         return null;
2629      if (skipIfEmpty == null)
2630         skipIfEmpty = client.isSkipEmptyHeaderData();
2631      if (serializer == null)
2632         serializer = client.getPartSerializer();
2633      return new SerializedHeader(name, value, getPartSerializerSession(serializer), schema, skipIfEmpty);
2634   }
2635
2636   /**
2637    * Constructs the {@link HttpRequestBase} object that ends up being passed to the client execute method.
2638    *
2639    * <p>
2640    * Subclasses can override this method to create their own request base objects.
2641    *
2642    * @param method The HTTP method.
2643    * @param uri The HTTP URI.
2644    * @param hasBody Whether the HTTP request has a body.
2645    * @return A new {@link HttpRequestBase} object.
2646    */
2647   protected HttpRequestBase createInnerRequest(String method, URI uri, boolean hasBody) {
2648      var req = hasBody ? new BasicHttpEntityRequestBase(this, method) : new BasicHttpRequestBase(this, method);
2649      req.setURI(uri);
2650      return req;
2651   }
2652
2653   /**
2654    * Creates a new query/form-data/path part.
2655    *
2656    * @param name The part name.
2657    * @param value The part value.
2658    * @param type The HTTP part type.
2659    * @param serializer The part serializer to use, or <jk>null</jk> to use the part serializer defined on the client.
2660    * @param schema Optional HTTP part schema to provide to the part serializer.
2661    * @param skipIfEmpty If <jk>true</jk>, empty string values will be ignored on the request.
2662    * @return A new part.
2663    */
2664   protected NameValuePair createPart(String name, Object value, HttpPartType type, HttpPartSerializer serializer, HttpPartSchema schema, Boolean skipIfEmpty) {
2665      if (ie(name))
2666         return null;
2667      if (skipIfEmpty == null) {
2668         if (type == QUERY)
2669            skipIfEmpty = client.isSkipEmptyQueryData();
2670         else if (type == FORMDATA)
2671            skipIfEmpty = client.isSkipEmptyFormData();
2672         else
2673            skipIfEmpty = false;
2674      }
2675      if (serializer == null)
2676         serializer = client.getPartSerializer();
2677      return new SerializedPart(name, value, type, getPartSerializerSession(serializer), schema, skipIfEmpty);
2678   }
2679
2680   @Override /* Overridden from MarshallingSession */
2681   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
2682      return super.properties()
2683         .a(ARG_client, client.properties())
2684         .a(PROP_ignoreErrors, ignoreErrors)
2685         .a(PROP_interceptors, interceptors)
2686         .a(PROP_requestBodySchema, contentSchema)
2687         .a(PROP_response, response)
2688         .a(PROP_serializer, serializer);
2689   }
2690
2691   RestRequest formDataArg(String name, Object value, HttpPartSchema schema, HttpPartSerializer serializer, boolean skipIfEmpty) {
2692      var isMulti = ie(name) || "*".equals(name) || value instanceof PartList || isNameValuePairArray(value);
2693
2694      if (! isMulti) {
2695         if (! (skipIfEmpty && ie(s(value))))
2696            return formData(createPart(name, value, FORMDATA, serializer, schema, skipIfEmpty));
2697         return this;
2698      }
2699
2700      List<NameValuePair> l = list();
2701
2702      if (HttpParts.canCast(value)) {
2703         l.add(HttpParts.cast(value));
2704      } else if (value instanceof PartList value2) {
2705         value2.forEach(l::add);
2706      } else if (value instanceof Collection<?> value3) {
2707         value3.forEach(x -> l.add(HttpParts.cast(x)));
2708      } else if (isArray(value)) {
2709         for (var i = 0; i < Array.getLength(value); i++)
2710            l.add(HttpParts.cast(Array.get(value, i)));
2711      } else if (value instanceof Map value2) {
2712         toMap(value2).forEach((k, v) -> l.add(createPart(s(k), v, FORMDATA, serializer, schema, skipIfEmpty)));
2713      } else if (isBean(value)) {
2714         toBeanMap(value).forEach((k, v) -> l.add(createPart(k, v, FORMDATA, serializer, schema, skipIfEmpty)));
2715      } else if (nn(value)) {
2716         formDataCustom(value);
2717         return this;
2718      }
2719
2720      if (skipIfEmpty)
2721         l.removeIf(x -> ie(x.getValue()));
2722
2723      formData.append(l);
2724
2725      return this;
2726   }
2727
2728   HttpPartSerializerSession getPartSerializerSession() {
2729      if (partSerializerSession == null)
2730         partSerializerSession = getPartSerializerSession(null);
2731      return partSerializerSession;
2732   }
2733
2734   RestRequest headerArg(String name, Object value, HttpPartSchema schema, HttpPartSerializer serializer, boolean skipIfEmpty) {
2735      var isMulti = ie(name) || "*".equals(name) || value instanceof HeaderList || isHeaderArray(value);
2736
2737      if (! isMulti) {
2738         if (! (skipIfEmpty && ie(s(value))))
2739            return header(createHeader(name, value, serializer, schema, skipIfEmpty));
2740         return this;
2741      }
2742
2743      List<Header> l = list();
2744
2745      if (HttpHeaders.canCast(value)) {
2746         l.add(HttpHeaders.cast(value));
2747      } else if (value instanceof HeaderList value2) {
2748         value2.forEach(l::add);
2749      } else if (value instanceof Collection<?> value3) {
2750         value3.forEach(x -> l.add(HttpHeaders.cast(x)));
2751      } else if (isArray(value)) {
2752         for (var i = 0; i < Array.getLength(value); i++)
2753            l.add(HttpHeaders.cast(Array.get(value, i)));
2754      } else if (value instanceof Map value2) {
2755         toMap(value2).forEach((k, v) -> l.add(createHeader(s(k), v, serializer, schema, skipIfEmpty)));
2756      } else if (isBean(value)) {
2757         toBeanMap(value).forEach((k, v) -> l.add(createHeader(k, v, serializer, schema, skipIfEmpty)));
2758      } else if (nn(value)) {
2759         throw rex("Invalid value type for header arg ''{0}'': {1}", name, cn(value));
2760      }
2761
2762      if (skipIfEmpty)
2763         l.removeIf(x -> ie(x.getValue()));
2764
2765      headerData.append(l);
2766
2767      return this;
2768   }

2772   RestRequest pathArg(String name, Object value, HttpPartSchema schema, HttpPartSerializer serializer) {
2773      var isMulti = ie(name) || "*".equals(name) || value instanceof PartList || isNameValuePairArray(value);
2774
2775      if (! isMulti)
2776         return pathData(createPart(name, value, PATH, serializer, schema, false));
2777
2778      List<NameValuePair> l = list();
2779
2780      if (HttpParts.canCast(value)) {
2781         l.add(HttpParts.cast(value));
2782      } else if (value instanceof PartList value2) {
2783         value2.forEach(l::add);
2784      } else if (value instanceof Collection<?> value3) {
2785         value3.forEach(x -> l.add(HttpParts.cast(x)));
2786      } else if (isArray(value)) {
2787         for (var i = 0; i < Array.getLength(value); i++)
2788            l.add(HttpParts.cast(Array.get(value, i)));
2789      } else if (value instanceof Map value2) {
2790         toMap(value2).forEach((k, v) -> l.add(createPart(s(k), v, PATH, serializer, schema, false)));
2791      } else if (isBean(value)) {
2792         toBeanMap(value).forEach((k, v) -> l.add(createPart(k, v, PATH, serializer, schema, false)));
2793      } else if (nn(value)) {
2794         throw rex("Invalid value type for path arg ''{0}'': {1}", name, cn(value));
2795      }
2796
2797      pathData.append(l);
2798
2799      return this;
2800   }
2801
2802   RestRequest queryArg(String name, Object value, HttpPartSchema schema, HttpPartSerializer serializer, boolean skipIfEmpty) {
2803      var isMulti = ie(name) || "*".equals(name) || value instanceof PartList || isNameValuePairArray(value);
2804
2805      if (! isMulti) {
2806         if (! (skipIfEmpty && ie(s(value))))
2807            return queryData(createPart(name, value, QUERY, serializer, schema, skipIfEmpty));
2808         return this;
2809      }
2810
2811      List<NameValuePair> l = list();
2812
2813      if (HttpParts.canCast(value)) {
2814         l.add(HttpParts.cast(value));
2815      } else if (value instanceof PartList value2) {
2816         value2.forEach(l::add);
2817      } else if (value instanceof Collection<?> value3) {
2818         value3.forEach(x -> l.add(HttpParts.cast(x)));
2819      } else if (isArray(value)) {
2820         for (var i = 0; i < Array.getLength(value); i++)
2821            l.add(HttpParts.cast(Array.get(value, i)));
2822      } else if (value instanceof Map value2) {
2823         toMap(value2).forEach((k, v) -> l.add(createPart(s(k), v, QUERY, serializer, schema, skipIfEmpty)));
2824      } else if (isBean(value)) {
2825         toBeanMap(value).forEach((k, v) -> l.add(createPart(k, v, QUERY, serializer, schema, skipIfEmpty)));
2826      } else if (nn(value)) {
2827         queryCustom(value);
2828         return this;
2829      }
2830
2831      if (skipIfEmpty)
2832         l.removeIf(x -> ie(x.getValue()));
2833
2834      queryData.append(l);
2835
2836      return this;
2837   }
2838
2839   /**
2840    * Resolves a {@link RecordStreamBody} body by invoking the caller's consumer against a
2841    * cursor opened over a temporary buffer, then sending the buffered bytes as the request
2842    * body.
2843    */
2844   @SuppressWarnings({"unchecked", "rawtypes"})
2845   private HttpEntity streamBodyEntity(RecordStreamBody body, Serializer serializer) {
2846      if (serializer == null)
2847         throw iaex("No serializer registered for cursor-streamed request body.");
2848      try {
2849         var consumer = body.getConsumer();
2850         var writerKind = body.getWriterKind();
2851
2852         if (writerKind == TokenWriter.class && !(serializer instanceof TokenWritable))
2853            throw iaex("Serializer ''{0}'' does not support the token-writer surface.", serializer.getClass().getName());
2854         if (writerKind == RecordWriter.class && !(serializer instanceof RecordWritable))
2855            throw iaex("Serializer ''{0}'' does not support the record-writer surface.", serializer.getClass().getName());
2856
2857         var buf = new ByteArrayOutputStream();
2858         Object output = serializer.isWriterSerializer()
2859            ? new OutputStreamWriter(buf, java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets.UTF_8)
2860            : buf;
2861
2862         try (var w = (writerKind == TokenWriter.class)
2863               ? ((TokenWritable) serializer).serializeTokens(output)
2864               : ((RecordWritable) serializer).serializeRecords(output)) {
2865            ((Consumer) consumer).accept(w);
2866            w.flush();
2867         }
2868         if (output instanceof Writer ow)
2869            ow.flush();
2870
2871         // Use the serializer's primary produces() media type if no explicit Content-Type set.
2872         var ct = serializer.getPrimaryMediaType();
2873         var contentType2 = ct != null ? ContentType.of(ct.toString()) : ContentType.APPLICATION_OCTET_STREAM;
2874         return byteArrayEntity(buf.toByteArray(), getRequestContentType(contentType2));
2875      } catch (IOException e) {
2876         throw rex(e, "I/O error streaming request body.");
2877      }
2878   }
2879}
